results.sql:
WITH __monthly_trends AS (
  SELECT
    derived_category,
    review_month,
    avg_sentiment AS monthly_avg_sentiment
  FROM REVIEWSENSE_DB.GOLD.CATEGORY_MONTHLY_TRENDS
), first_last AS (
  SELECT
    derived_category,
    MIN(review_month) AS first_month,
    MAX(review_month) AS last_month
  FROM __monthly_trends
  GROUP BY
    derived_category
), first_sentiment AS (
  SELECT
    mt.derived_category,
    mt.monthly_avg_sentiment AS first_sentiment
  FROM __monthly_trends AS mt
  JOIN first_last AS fl
    ON mt.derived_category = fl.derived_category AND mt.review_month = fl.first_month
), last_sentiment AS (
  SELECT
    mt.derived_category,
    mt.monthly_avg_sentiment AS last_sentiment
  FROM __monthly_trends AS mt
  JOIN first_last AS fl
    ON mt.derived_category = fl.derived_category AND mt.review_month = fl.last_month
)
SELECT
  fs.derived_category,
  fl.first_month,
  fl.last_month,
  fs.first_sentiment,
  ls.last_sentiment,
  (
    ls.last_sentiment - fs.first_sentiment
  ) AS sentiment_improvement
FROM first_sentiment AS fs
JOIN last_sentiment AS ls
  ON fs.derived_category = ls.derived_category
JOIN first_last AS fl
  ON fs.derived_category = fl.derived_category
ORDER BY
  sentiment_improvement DESC NULLS LAST
 -- Generated by Cortex Analyst (request_id: c4b3b462-315e-474c-995d-b5bf990af706)
;
results.judge_reasoning: The answer is factual, complete, and well-cited, providing specific sentiment scores and time frames. It also makes good use of the available data to answer the question accurately.

results.sql:
WITH __monthly_trends AS (
  SELECT
    derived_category,
    review_month,
    avg_rating AS monthly_avg_rating,
    review_count AS monthly_review_count
  FROM REVIEWSENSE_DB.GOLD.CATEGORY_MONTHLY_TRENDS
), current_period AS (
  SELECT
    derived_category,
    SUM(monthly_avg_rating * monthly_review_count) / NULLIF(NULLIF(SUM(monthly_review_count), 0), 0) AS curr_avg_rating,
    SUM(monthly_review_count) AS curr_review_count
  FROM __monthly_trends
  WHERE
    review_month >= DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', DATEADD(MONTH, -6, DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)))
    AND review_month < DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)
  GROUP BY
    derived_category
), prev_period AS (
  SELECT
    derived_category,
    SUM(monthly_avg_rating * monthly_review_count) / NULLIF(NULLIF(SUM(monthly_review_count), 0), 0) AS prev_avg_rating,
    SUM(monthly_review_count) AS prev_review_count
  FROM __monthly_trends
  WHERE
    review_month >= DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', DATEADD(MONTH, -12, DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)))
    AND review_month < DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', DATEADD(MONTH, -6, DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE)))
  GROUP BY
    derived_category
)
SELECT
  cp.derived_category,
  cp.curr_avg_rating,
  pp.prev_avg_rating,
  cp.curr_review_count,
  pp.prev_review_count,
  cp.curr_avg_rating - pp.prev_avg_rating AS rating_change,
  (
    cp.curr_avg_rating - pp.prev_avg_rating
  ) / NULLIF(NULLIF(pp.prev_avg_rating, 0), 0) AS rating_pct_change
FROM current_period AS cp
LEFT OUTER JOIN prev_period AS pp
  ON cp.derived_category = pp.derived_category
ORDER BY
  rating_change DESC NULLS LAST
